title: Idioms & Expressions Set 2 — Advanced Business Idioms
titleTr: Deyimler ve Ifadeler Set 2 — Ileri Is Deyimleri
description: Master 30 advanced business idioms used in C-suite conversations, investor meetings, and high-stakes negotiations. Learn when to use them, when NOT to use them, and the cultural context behind each metaphor.
descriptionTr: Ust duzey yonetim konusmalarinda, yatirimci toplantIlarinda ve yuksek riskli muzakerelerde kullanilan 30 ileri is deyimini ogren. Ne zaman kullanimali, ne zaman KULLANMAMALI ve her metaforun arkasindaki kulturel baglami kavra.

vocabulary.example: This campaign didn't move the needle — we need a bolder strategy.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Bu kampanya gozle gorulur bir fark yaratmadi — daha cesur bir stratejiye ihtiyacimiz var.
vocabulary.extra: The CEO asked, 'Will this initiative actually move the needle on revenue?'
vocabulary.extraTr: CEO sordu: 'Bu girisim gelirde gercekten gozle gorulur bir fark yaratacak mi?'

vocabulary.turkish: kolay elde edilebilir firsat / basit kazanim
vocabulary.example: Let's go after the low-hanging fruit first before tackling complex markets.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Karmasik pazarlara girmeden once kolay elde edilebilir firsatlari yakalayalim.
vocabulary.extra: Investors love hearing about low-hanging fruit — it shows quick wins are possible.
vocabulary.extraTr: Yatirimcilar kolay kazanimlar duymaktan hoslanir — hizli zaferler mumkun oldugunu gosterir.

vocabulary.turkish: imkansizi yapmaya calismak / gereksiz yere kapsamlI olmak
vocabulary.example: We're not trying to boil the ocean — let's focus on three core markets.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Imkansizi denemeye calismiyoruz — uc temel pazara odaklanalim.
vocabulary.extra: Don't boil the ocean with this project — keep the scope realistic.
vocabulary.extraTr: Bu projede imkansizi deneme — kapsami gercekci tut.

vocabulary.turkish: koru korune inanmak / sorgulamadan kabul etmek
vocabulary.example: I'm not ready to drink the Kool-Aid on this strategy until I see real data.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Gercek veri gorene kadar bu stratejiye koru korune inanmaya hazir degilim.
vocabulary.extra: Be careful: this idiom has a dark origin and can sound dismissive in some contexts.
vocabulary.extraTr: Dikkat: bu deyimin karanlik bir kokeni var ve bazi baglamlarda kucumseyici gorunebilir.

vocabulary.word: put all your eggs in one basket
vocabulary.pronunciation: put ol yor EGZ in van BES-kit
vocabulary.turkish: tum riskini tek bir seye yatirmak
vocabulary.example: Don't put all your eggs in one basket — diversify your revenue streams.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Tum riskini tek bir seye yatirma — gelir kaynaklarini cesitlendir.
vocabulary.extra: The board warned against putting all our eggs in one basket with the European market.
vocabulary.extraTr: Yonetim kurulu Avrupa pazariyla tum riskin tek yere yatirilmasina karsi uyardi.

vocabulary.example: The elephant in the room is that our largest client is about to leave.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Herkesin bildigi ama konusmadigi sorun, en buyuk musterimizin ayrilmak uzere olmasi.
vocabulary.extra: Let's address the elephant in the room — our profit margins are shrinking.
vocabulary.extraTr: Herkesin farkinda oldugu sorunu konusalim — kar marjlarimiz daralIyor.

vocabulary.turkish: iki ucu keskIn bicak / hem iyi hem kotu
vocabulary.example: Rapid growth is a double-edged sword — it brings revenue but strains resources.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Hizli buyume iki ucu keskin bicaktir — gelir getirir ama kaynaklari zorlar.
vocabulary.extra: Social media marketing is a double-edged sword — great for visibility, risky for reputation.
vocabulary.extraTr: Sosyal medya pazarlamasi iki ucu keskin bicaktir — gorünurluk icin harika, itibar icin riskli.

vocabulary.word: hit the nail on the head
vocabulary.pronunciation: hit dı NEYL on dı HED
vocabulary.turkish: tam isabetle soylemek / tam dogrusunu bulmak
vocabulary.example: You hit the nail on the head — our pricing strategy is the real problem.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Tam isabetli soyledin — fiyatlandirma stratejimiz asil sorun.
vocabulary.extra: When she said the issue was communication, not budget, she hit the nail on the head.
vocabulary.extraTr: Sorunun butce degil iletisim oldugunu soylediginde tam dogrusunu buldu.

vocabulary.turkish: yanlis yerde aramak / yanlis adrese basvurmak
vocabulary.example: If you think cutting marketing will save costs, you're barking up the wrong tree.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Pazarlamayi kesmenin maliyetleri dusureCegini dusunuyorsan yanlis yerde ariyorsun.
vocabulary.extra: The auditors were barking up the wrong tree — the discrepancy was in logistics, not finance.
vocabulary.extraTr: Denetciler yanlis yerde ariyordu — tutarsizlik finansta degil lojistikteydi.

vocabulary.turkish: birini gunah kecisi yapmak / birini sIrtindan bicaklamak
vocabulary.example: He threw his entire team under the bus during the board meeting to save himself.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Kendini kurtarmak icin yonetim kurulu toplantisinda tum ekibini gunah kecisi yapti.
vocabulary.extra: A good leader never throws their team under the bus, even when things go wrong.
vocabulary.extraTr: Iyi bir lider, isler ters gitse bile ekibini asla gunah kecisi yapmaz.

vocabulary.turkish: satirlarin arasini okumak / ima edileni anlamak
vocabulary.example: The client said 'we'll think about it' — read between the lines, they're not interested.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Musteri 'dusunecegiz' dedi — satirlarin arasini oku, ilgilenmiyorlar.
vocabulary.extra: In C-suite emails, you must read between the lines — what's not said is often more important.
vocabulary.extraTr: Ust yonetim e-postalarinda satirlarin arasini okumalisin — soylenmeyen genellikle daha onemlidir.

vocabulary.example: The new ERP system has a steep learning curve, but it's worth the investment.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Yeni ERP sisteminin zor bir ogrenme sureci var ama yatirima deger.
vocabulary.extra: Joining a startup means accepting a steep learning curve from day one.
vocabulary.extraTr: Bir startup'a katilmak ilk gunden zor bir ogrenme surecini kabul etmek demektir.

vocabulary.example: Let's cut to the chase — do we have the budget to proceed or not?
vocabulary.exampleTr: Sadede gelelim — devam etmek icin butcemiz var mi yok mu?
vocabulary.extra: Investors appreciate it when you cut to the chase in pitch meetings.
vocabulary.extraTr: Yatirimcilar sunum toplantIlarinda sadede gelmenizi takdir eder.

vocabulary.word: get your ducks in a row
vocabulary.pronunciation: get yor DAKS in ı ROU
vocabulary.example: Before the investor meeting, make sure you get your ducks in a row.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Yatirimci toplantisIndan once her seyin hazir olduguna emin ol.
vocabulary.extra: The CFO told us to get our ducks in a row before the audit begins.
vocabulary.extraTr: CFO denetim baslamadan once her seyi duzenli hale getirmemizi soyledi.

vocabulary.turkish: oyunda paylari olmak / kisisel risk almak
vocabulary.example: I trust his advice because he has skin in the game — he invested his own money.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Tavsiyesine guveniyorum cunku oyunda paylari var — kendi parasini yatirdi.
vocabulary.extra: Investors prefer founders who have skin in the game, not just ideas.
vocabulary.extraTr: Yatirimcilar sadece fikirleri degil, oyunda paylari olan kuruculari tercih eder.

vocabulary.turkish: esit sartlar saglamak / rekabeti adil hale getirmek
vocabulary.example: Open-source technology helps level the playing field for smaller companies.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Acik kaynak teknolojisi kucuk sirketler icin esit sartlar saglamaya yardimci olur.
vocabulary.extra: Government regulations aim to level the playing field between local and international firms.
vocabulary.extraTr: Devlet duzenlemeleri yerel ve uluslararasi firmalar arasinda esit sartlar saglamayi amaclar.

vocabulary.example: The competitor's new product launch has raised the stakes for everyone in the industry.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Rakibin yeni urun lansmanI sektordeki herkes icin bahisleri yUkseltti.
vocabulary.extra: When they doubled their investment, it raised the stakes significantly.
vocabulary.extraTr: Yatirimlarini ikiye katladiklarinda bahisler onemli olcude yukseldi.

vocabulary.example: The client keeps shifting the goalposts — every time we deliver, they add new requirements.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Musteri surekli kurallari degistiriyor — her teslim ettigimizde yeni gereksinimler ekliyor.
vocabulary.extra: It's frustrating when management shifts the goalposts on performance targets.
vocabulary.extraTr: Yonetimin performans hedeflerinde kurallari degistirmesi sinir bozucu.

vocabulary.example: Before committing fully, let's test the waters with a soft launch in one city.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Tam olarak taahhut etmeden once, bir sehirde yumusak lansmaNla nabiz yoklayalim.
vocabulary.extra: We tested the waters in the UK market before expanding across Europe.
vocabulary.extraTr: Avrupa genelinde genislemeden once Ingiltere pazarinda nabiz yokladik.

vocabulary.example: Companies that weathered the storm during the recession emerged stronger.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Durgunluk sirasinda firtinayI atlatan sirketler daha guclu cikti.
vocabulary.extra: Our cash reserves helped us weather the storm when supply chains collapsed.
vocabulary.extraTr: Nakit rezervlerimiz tedarik zincirleri coktuNde firtinayI atlatmamiza yardimci oldu.

vocabulary.example: The new marketing strategy turned the tide — sales jumped 40% in Q3.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Yeni pazarlama stratejisi gidisati degistirdi — satislar 3. ceyrekte %40 artti.
vocabulary.extra: One brilliant hire can turn the tide for a struggling department.
vocabulary.extraTr: Tek bir parlak ise alim zorlu bir departman icin gidisati degistirebilir.

vocabulary.example: We need to bridge the gap between our current capabilities and market expectations.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Mevcut yeteneklerimiz ile pazar beklentileri arasindaki arayi kapatmamiz gerekiyor.
vocabulary.extra: Training programs help bridge the gap between junior and senior employees.
vocabulary.extraTr: Egitim programlari ast ve ust duzey calisanlar arasindaki farki gidermeye yardimci olur.

vocabulary.example: Without proper project management, important tasks will fall through the cracks.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Duzgun proje yonetimi olmadan onemli gorevler gozden kacar.
vocabulary.extra: Customer complaints were falling through the cracks because nobody owned the process.
vocabulary.extraTr: Musteri sikayetleri gozden kaciyordu cunku kimse surecin sahibi degildi.

vocabulary.example: Our R&D team constantly pushes the envelope with breakthrough innovations.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Ar-Ge ekibimiz cigiR acan yeniliklerle surekli sinirlari zorluyor.
vocabulary.extra: If we don't push the envelope, our competitors will overtake us.
vocabulary.extraTr: Sinirlari zorlamazsak rakiplerimiz bizi geCer.

vocabulary.word: separate the wheat from the chaff
vocabulary.pronunciation: SE-pı-reyt dı WIT from dı CEF
vocabulary.turkish: iyiyi kotuden ayirmak / degerleniyi degerSizden ayiklamak
vocabulary.example: The due diligence process helps separate the wheat from the chaff among potential investments.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Durum tespit sureci potansiyel yatirimlar arasinda iyiyi kotuden ayirmaya yardimci olur.
vocabulary.extra: A good recruiter can separate the wheat from the chaff in the first interview.
vocabulary.extraTr: Iyi bir ise alim uzmani ilk mulakatta iyiyi kotuden ayirabilir.

vocabulary.example: Even if you disagree, don't burn bridges — you may need these contacts later.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Katilmasaniz bile koprulerI yakmayin — bu baglantilara sonra ihtiyaciniz olabilir.
vocabulary.extra: He burned bridges with every company he left, which hurt his reputation.
vocabulary.extraTr: Ayrildigin her sirketle koprulerini yakti, bu da itibarini zedeledi.

vocabulary.turkish: kotu bir durumun iyi tarafi / umut isigi
vocabulary.example: The silver lining of the market crash was that it forced us to innovate.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Piyasa cokusunun iyi tarafi bizi yenilik yapmaya zorlamsiydi.
vocabulary.extra: Every crisis has a silver lining if you look for opportunities.
vocabulary.extraTr: Firsatlari ararsaniz her krizin bir iyi tarafi vardir.

vocabulary.example: The compliance issues we found are just the tip of the iceberg.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Buldugumuz uyumluluk sorunlari buzdaginin sadece gorunen kismi.
vocabulary.extra: These customer complaints are the tip of the iceberg — deeper problems exist.
vocabulary.extraTr: Bu musteri sikayetleri buzdaginin gorunen kismi — daha derin sorunlar var.

vocabulary.turkish: zor bir siparis / gerceklestirilmesi guc talep
vocabulary.example: Doubling revenue in six months is a tall order, but not impossible.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Alti ayda geliri ikiye katlamak zor bir talep ama imkansiz degil.
vocabulary.extra: The board gave us a tall order — reduce costs by 30% without layoffs.
vocabulary.extraTr: Yonetim kurulu bize zor bir gorev verdi — isten cikarma olmadan maliyetleri %30 azalt.

vocabulary.example: Delays in government approvals are par for the course when expanding internationally.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Uluslararasi genislemede devlet onaylarindaki gecikmeler beklenen bir durum.
vocabulary.extra: Budget overruns on large projects? That's par for the course, unfortunately.
vocabulary.extraTr: Buyuk projelerde butce aSimlari mi? Maalesef beklenen bir durum.

grammar.title: Idiom Mastery — Metaphor Analysis, Register Awareness & Cultural Context
grammar.titleTr: Deyim Ustlaligi — Metafor Analizi, Uslup Farkindaligi ve Kulturel Baglam
grammar.explanation: At C1 level, knowing an idiom's meaning is not enough. You must understand the metaphor behind it, know which register it belongs to (casual, business, formal), recognize when NOT to use idioms (legal documents, academic writing), and understand the cultural context. Overusing idioms sounds unnatural — a C1 speaker uses them strategically, not constantly.
grammar.explanationTr: C1 seviyesinde bir deyimin anlamini bilmek yeterli degildir. Arkasindaki metaforu anlamalisin, hangi usluba ait oldugunu bilmelisin (gundelik, is, resmi), deyimleri ne zaman KULLANMAMAN gerektigini (hukuki belgeler, akademik yazi) tanimalisin ve kulturel baglami kavramalisin. Deyimleri asiri kullanmak dogal olmaz — C1 konusmaci onlari surekli degil stratejik kullanir.
table: Metaphor Source: Nature/Animals → 'weather the storm,' 'the elephant in the room,' 'barking up the wrong tree'
Natural metaphors feel universal but are English-specific. A Turk might say 'araba devrilince yol gosteren cok olur' instead.
Knowing the metaphor source helps you remember AND use the idiom correctly.
Understand the IMAGE behind the idiom, not just the translation.
Metaphor Source: Games/Sports → 'move the needle,' 'level the playing field,' 'par for the course,' 'raise the stakes'
Sports idioms are extremely common in American business English because of competitive culture.
Use these in presentations and meetings to sound natural and confident.
American business culture is competitive — sports metaphors reflect this mindset.
Register: Business Casual → 'cut to the chase,' 'low-hanging fruit,' 'get your ducks in a row'
Perfect for internal meetings, team calls, and strategy sessions. NOT for legal contracts or academic papers.
'Let's cut to the chase' in a board meeting = fine. In a legal brief = inappropriate.
Idioms LOWER formality — use them to build rapport, not in formal written documents.
Register: Formal Business → 'a double-edged sword,' 'bridge the gap,' 'tip of the iceberg'
These work in presentations, reports, and investor communications because they sound analytical.
'This growth is a double-edged sword' sounds thoughtful in a quarterly report.
Some idioms ELEVATE your speech — they show critical thinking ability.
When NOT to use idioms → legal documents, academic writing, cross-cultural communication with non-native speakers
Idioms don't translate — 'boil the ocean' means nothing to a Japanese or German colleague who hasn't learned it.
In international contexts, replace idioms with clear language: 'let's focus' instead of 'low-hanging fruit.'
Overusing idioms with non-native speakers creates confusion, not connection.
Cultural Warning: Sensitive idioms → 'drink the Kool-Aid' (Jonestown massacre reference), 'throw under the bus' (aggressive imagery)
Know the origin stories. Some idioms carry historical weight that may offend.
'Drink the Kool-Aid' in a startup pitch could seem tone-deaf to culturally aware investors.
Research idiom origins — it shows cultural intelligence and avoids awkward moments.
Strategic Usage: Mix idioms with formal language for C1 impact
'The compliance issues we found are, frankly, just the tip of the iceberg — our full audit reveals systemic gaps.'
One well-placed idiom per paragraph or speech section. Never chain multiple idioms together.
C1 rule: ONE idiom per key point. More than that sounds like you're trying too hard.

tips.en: Never chain idioms: 'Let's cut to the chase, get our ducks in a row, and avoid boiling the ocean' sounds ridiculous. Use ONE per key point.
tips.tr: Deyimleri asla zincirleme: 'Sadede gelelim, her seyi duzenleyelim ve imkansizi denemeyelim' komik durur. Her ana nokta icin BIR tane kullan.

tips.en: In investor meetings, 'skin in the game' and 'move the needle' are gold — investors use these constantly. Match their language.
tips.tr: Yatirimci toplantIlarinda 'skin in the game' ve 'move the needle' altin degerinde — yatirimcilar bunlari surekli kullanir. Onlarin dilini kullan.

tips.en: When presenting bad news, use 'tip of the iceberg' or 'elephant in the room' to sound honest and strategic, not panicky.
tips.tr: Kotu haber verirken 'tip of the iceberg' veya 'elephant in the room' kullan — panikci degil durustve stratejik gorunursun.

tips.en: Replace idioms in cross-cultural contexts: Instead of 'low-hanging fruit,' say 'quick wins.' Instead of 'boil the ocean,' say 'keep our scope focused.'
tips.tr: Kulturlerarasi baglamlarda deyimleri degistir: 'Low-hanging fruit' yerine 'quick wins.' 'Boil the ocean' yerine 'keep our scope focused.'

tips.en: 'Par for the course' is perfect for normalizing setbacks: 'Supply chain delays? That's par for the course in Q4.' Calm, experienced, professional.
tips.tr: 'Par for the course' aksilikleri normallestiRmek icin mukemmel: 'Tedarik zinciri gecikmeleri mi? 4. ceyrekte beklenen bir durum.' Sakin, deneyimli, profesyonel.

tips.en: Test before you use: If you're unsure whether an idiom fits the context, use plain language instead. Misusing an idiom is worse than not using one.
tips.tr: Kullanmadan once test et: Bir deyimin baglama uyup uymadigIndan emin degilsen, onun yerine sade dil kullan. Bir deyimi yanlis kullanmak hic kullanmamaktan kotudur.

reading.text:
THE PITCH THAT CHANGED EVERYTHING — A VC Meeting Transcript

Last quarter, NovaTech Solutions walked into a high-stakes investor meeting with Horizon Ventures. The stakes couldn't have been higher — $25 million in Series C funding was on the line, and the competition was fierce.

Eyüp, now NovaTech's Chief Strategy Officer, opened the pitch: 'Let me cut to the chase. We're not here to boil the ocean. We've identified the low-hanging fruit in the Southeast Asian logistics market, and our data shows we can move the needle within 12 months.'

The lead investor, Ms. Whitfield, pushed back immediately: 'Every startup claims they can move the needle. What makes you different? And let's address the elephant in the room — your burn rate doubled last quarter.'

Eyüp didn't flinch: 'You've hit the nail on the head, and I appreciate your directness. Yes, our burn rate increased, but that's the tip of the iceberg — in a good way. We invested heavily in infrastructure that will reduce operational costs by 35% within two quarters. It's a double-edged sword: higher short-term spending for exponentially lower long-term costs.'

Ms. Whitfield continued: 'And the regulatory risks in Southeast Asia? That seems like a tall order for a company your size.'

'Regulatory delays are par for the course in emerging markets,' Eyüp responded confidently. 'We've already tested the waters with a pilot program in Thailand, and the results exceeded every benchmark. We're not putting all our eggs in one basket — we have simultaneous pilots in three countries. And crucially, I have skin in the game. I've invested my own capital alongside the team.'

The room shifted. Ms. Whitfield leaned forward: 'The market conditions are tough. How do you plan to weather the storm if the economic downturn continues?'

Eyüp smiled: 'Our cash reserves give us an 18-month runway. But more importantly, we've built systems that separate the wheat from the chaff in our product pipeline. We know exactly which features push the envelope and which ones fall through the cracks. We don't burn bridges with partners, and we never shift the goalposts on our team.'

Horizon Ventures committed $25 million that afternoon. The silver lining of a tough fundraising market was that it forced NovaTech to sharpen every aspect of their pitch. Sometimes, weathering the storm is what turns the tide.
reading.textTr:
HER SEYI DEGISTIREN SUNUM — Bir Risk Sermayesi Toplanti Tutanagi

Gecen ceyrek NovaTech Solutions, Horizon Ventures ile yuksek riskli bir yatirimci toplantisina girdi. Bahisler daha yuksek olamazdi — 25 milyon dolarlik Seri C finansman sozdeydi ve rekabet sertti.

Artik NovaTech'in Strateji Muduru olan Eyup, sunumu acti: 'Sadede geleyim. Imkansizi denemeye burada degiliz. Guneydogu Asya lojistik pazarindaki kolay kazanimlari belirledik ve verilerimiz 12 ay icinde gozle gorulur fark yaratabileceGimizi gosteriyor.'

Bas yatirimci Bayan Whitfield hemen itiraz etti: 'Her startup gozle gorulur fark yaratabilecegini iddia ediyor. Sizi farkli yapan ne? Ve herkesin bildigi sorunu konusalim — yakim oraniniz gecen ceyrek ikiye katlandi.'

Eyup yilmadi: 'Tam dogrusunu buldunuz ve acikliginizi takdir ediyorum. Evet, yakim oranImiz artti ama bu buzdaginin sadece gorunen kismi — iyi anlamda. Iki ceyrek icinde operasyonel maliyetleri %35 azaltacak altyapiya yogun yatirim yaptik. Iki ucu keskin bicak: uzun vadede katlanarak dusen maliyetler icin daha yuksek kisa vadeli harcama.'

Bayan Whitfield devam etti: 'Peki Guneydogu Asya'daki duzenleyici riskler? Sizin buyuklugünuzdeki bir sirket icin zor bir gorev gibi gorunuyor.'

'Gelisen pazarlarda duzenleyici gecikmeler beklenen bir durum,' diye guvenle yanit verdi Eyup. 'Tayland'da bir pilot programla zaten nabiz yokladik ve sonuclar her referans degeri asti. Tum riskinizi tek yere yatirmiyoruz — uc ulkede esZamanli pilot programlarimiz var. Ve en onemlisi, oyunda paylarim var. Ekiple birlikte kendi sermayemi yatirdim.'

Odanin havasi degisti. Bayan Whitfield one egildi: 'Piyasa kosullari zor. Ekonomik durgunluk devam ederse firtinayI nasil atlatmayi planliyorsunuz?'

Eyup gulumsedi: 'Nakit rezervlerimiz bize 18 aylik pist veriyor. Ama daha da onemlisi, urun hattimizda iyiyi kotuden ayiran sistemler kurduk. Tam olarak hangi ozelliklerin sinirlari zorladiginI ve hangilerinin gozden kactigini biliyoruz. Ortaklarla kopru yakmayiz ve ekibimizde kurallari oyun ortasinda degistirmeyiz.'

Horizon Ventures o oGleden sonra 25 milyon dolar taahhut etti. Zor bir finansman piyasasinin iyi tarafi NovaTech'i sunumlarinin her yonunu keskinlestirmeye zorlmasiydi. Bazen firtinayI atlatmak gidisati degistiren seydir.

questions.question: What idiom did Eyüp use to explain NovaTech's focused approach instead of trying to do everything?
questions.questionTr: Eyup NovaTech'in her seyi yapmaya calismak yerine odakli yaklasimini aciklamak icin hangi deyimi kullanmisti?

questions.question: What was 'the elephant in the room' that Ms. Whitfield pointed out?
questions.questionTr: Bayan Whitfield'in isaret ettigi 'herkesin bildigi sorun' neydi?

questions.question: How did Eyüp demonstrate 'having skin in the game'?
What was the 'silver lining' of the tough fundraising market according to the text?
questions.questionTr: Metne gore zor finansman piyasasinin 'iyi tarafi' neydi?
options: It forced NovaTech to sharpen their pitch

questions.question: Which idiom pair did Eyüp use to describe NovaTech's integrity with partners and team?
questions.questionTr: Eyup ortaklar ve ekiple olan durustlugunu tanimlamak icin hangi deyim ciftini kullanmisti?
options: weather the storm / turn the tide
tip of the iceberg / double-edged sword

fillBlanks.sentence: Let's ___ to the chase — we don't have time for small talk.
The compliance issues we found are just the ___ of the iceberg.
fillBlanks.hint: Buzdaginin gorunen kismi — ___ of the iceberg

fillBlanks.sentence: Don't put all your ___ in one basket — diversify your revenue streams.
fillBlanks.hint: Tum riskinizi tek yere yatirmayin — put all your ___ in one basket

fillBlanks.sentence: We need to address the ___ in the room before moving forward.
fillBlanks.hint: Herkesin bildigi sorun — the ___ in the room

fillBlanks.sentence: Rapid growth is a double-edged ___ — it brings revenue but strains resources.
fillBlanks.hint: Iki ucu keskin bicak — a double-edged ___

fillBlanks.sentence: Let's go after the low-hanging ___ first before tackling harder markets.
Before the investor meeting, make sure you get your ___ in a row.
fillBlanks.hint: Her seyi duzenlemek — get your ___ in a row

fillBlanks.sentence: I trust his advice because he has ___ in the game.
fillBlanks.hint: Oyunda paylari olmak — have ___ in the game

fillBlanks.sentence: Budget overruns on large projects? That's ___ for the course.
We tested the ___ with a pilot program before fully committing.
One brilliant hire can ___ the tide for a struggling department.
Without proper tracking, important tasks will ___ through the cracks.
Even if you disagree, don't ___ bridges — you may need these contacts later.
The silver ___ of the crisis was that it forced us to innovate.
Our R&D team constantly pushes the ___ with breakthrough innovations.

multipleChoice.question: 'Boil the ocean' ne zaman kullanilir?
options: Imkansizi veya gereksiz yere kapsamliyi denediginde

multipleChoice.question: Hangi baglam icin deyim kullanimi UYGUN DEGILDIR?
'She hit the nail on the head with her analysis.' Ne anlama gelir?
'Read between the lines' ne demek?
options: Satirlarin arasini okumak / ima edileni anlamak

multipleChoice.question: 'A steep learning curve' hangi durumu tanimlar?
C1 seviyesinde deyim kullanimi icin en iyi kural nedir?
options: Bir ana nokta basina BIR deyim yeterli

multipleChoice.question: 'Level the playing field' ne anlama gelir?
'Throw someone under the bus' ne anlama gelir?
'Shift the goalposts' ne zaman kullanilir?
options: Kurallar veya hedefler oyun ortasinda degistirildiginde

multipleChoice.question: Kulturlerarasi iletisimde 'low-hanging fruit' yerine ne demek daha iyi?
'Separate the wheat from the chaff' en yakin anlam?

matching.left: put all eggs in one basket
hit the nail on the head
get your ducks in a row
separate the wheat from the chaff

sentenceOrder.correct: Let's cut to the chase we need results
Don't put all your eggs in one basket
The compliance issues are just the tip of the iceberg
We need to address the elephant in the room
She hit the nail on the head with her analysis
Our team constantly pushes the envelope with innovation
Companies that weathered the storm emerged stronger
I trust him because he has skin in the game
Even if you disagree don't burn bridges
A good recruiter separates the wheat from the chaff

translation.turkish: Sadede gelelim — bu butceyle devam edebilir miyiz?
translation.english: Let's cut to the chase — can we proceed with this budget?
acceptAlso: Cut to the chase — do we have the budget to proceed?

translation.turkish: Onlarin yeni urunu sektordeki herkes icin bahisleri yUkseltti.
translation.english: Their new product raised the stakes for everyone in the industry.
acceptAlso: Their new product launch raised the stakes for the whole industry.

translation.turkish: Buldugumuz sorunlar buzdaginin sadece gorunen kismi.
translation.english: The problems we found are just the tip of the iceberg.
acceptAlso: The issues we discovered are just the tip of the iceberg.

translation.english: I trust founders who have skin in the game.
acceptAlso: I trust founders with skin in the game.

translation.english: Let's go after the low-hanging fruit first.
acceptAlso: Let's target the low-hanging fruit first.

translation.english: Don't burn bridges when delivering bad news.
acceptAlso: Don't burn your bridges when you deliver bad news.

translation.english: We need to address the elephant in the room.
acceptAlso: Let's address the elephant in the room.

translation.turkish: Hizli buyume iki ucu keskin bicaktir.
translation.english: Rapid growth is a double-edged sword.
acceptAlso: Fast growth is a double-edged sword.

translation.turkish: Musterimizde nabiz yoklamak icin pilot program baslatik.
translation.english: We launched a pilot program to test the waters with our client.
acceptAlso: We started a pilot program to test the waters.

translation.turkish: Alti ayda geliri ikiye katlamak zor bir talep.
translation.english: Doubling revenue in six months is a tall order.
acceptAlso: It's a tall order to double revenue in six months.

dialog.title: Eyüp's High-Stakes Investor Meeting
dialog.context: Eyup, NovaTech Solutions'in Strateji Muduru olarak Horizon Ventures'in Seri C yatirim turunda 25 milyon dolarlik sunum yapiyor. Oda: CEO Clara, Lead investor Ms. Whitfield, Teknik yatirimci Mr. Zhang, ve CFO David. Her konusmaci farkli bir perspektiften sorguluyor.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Good morning, everyone. Thank you for making the trip. Before we begin, I want to cut to the chase — we've reviewed dozens of pitch decks this quarter. What makes NovaTech different? Convince me in sixty seconds.
lines.textTr: Gunaydin millet. Geldiginiz icin tesekkurler. Baslamadan once sadede gelmek istiyorum — bu ceyrek duZinelerce sunum inceledik. NovaTech'i farkli yapan ne? Beni altmis saniyede ikna edin.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Thank you, Ms. Whitfield. Here's our sixty-second pitch: We've identified the low-hanging fruit in Southeast Asian logistics — a $4.2 billion underserved market. Our pilot in Thailand moved the needle: 340% user growth in 90 days. And I have skin in the game — I've invested $200,000 of my own capital.
lines.textTr: Tesekkurler Bayan Whitfield. Iste altmis sanyelik sunumumuz: Guneydogu Asya lojistiginde kolay kazanimlari belirledik — 4.2 milyar dolarlik yetersiz hizmet verilen bir pazar. Tayland'daki pilotumuz gozle gorulur fark yaratti: 90 gunde %340 kullanici buyumesi. Ve oyunda paylarim var — 200.000 dolar kendi sermayemi yatirdim.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Impressive numbers. But let's address the elephant in the room — your burn rate doubled last quarter. That's concerning for a Series C company.
lines.textTr: Etkileyici rakamlar. Ama herkesin bildigi sorunu konusalim — yakim oraniniz gecen ceyrek ikiye katlandi. Seri C bir sirket icin bu endise verici.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: You've hit the nail on the head, and I won't sugarcoat it. Yes, burn rate increased. But what you're seeing is the tip of the iceberg — in a positive sense. We invested in infrastructure that will reduce operational costs by 35% within two quarters. It's a double-edged sword: higher short-term spend, exponentially lower long-term costs.
lines.textTr: Tam dogrusunu buldunuz ve tatlandirmayacagim. Evet, yakim orani artti. Ama gordugunuz buzdaginin gorunen kismi — olumlu anlamda. Iki ceyrek icinde operasyonel maliyetleri %35 azaltacak altyapiya yatirim yaptik. Iki ucu keskin bicak: daha yuksek kisa vadeli harcama, katlanarak dusen uzun vadeli maliyetler.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: I want to talk about the technical side. Your platform architecture — is it built to scale, or are you going to need another $20 million just to handle growth? I've seen too many startups bark up the wrong tree by building for features instead of scale.
lines.textTr: Teknik taraftan konusmak istiyorum. Platform mimariniz — olceklenecek sekilde mi yapilmis, yoksa sadece buyumeyi yonetmek icin 20 milyon dolara daha mi ihtiyaciniz olacak? Olcek yerine ozellikler icin yaparak yanlis yerde arayan cok fazla startup gordum.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Excellent question, Mr. Zhang. We're not boiling the ocean with features. Our architecture is microservices-based, designed to scale horizontally. We've already tested the waters with 50,000 concurrent users, and the system handled it with zero downtime. We push the envelope on reliability, not complexity.
lines.textTr: Mukemmel soru Bay Zhang. Ozelliklerle imkansizi denemeye calismiyoruz. Mimarimiz mikro hizmet tabanli, yatay olarak olceklenecek sekilde tasarlanmis. 50.000 esZamanli kullaniclyla zaten nabiz yokladik ve sistem sifir kesinti ile basettti. Karmasiklikta degil guvenilirlikte sinirlari zorluyoruz.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: What about the competitive landscape? Logistify just raised $40 million. They're your direct competitor. How do you level the playing field against someone with deeper pockets?
lines.textTr: Rekabet ortami ne durumda? Logistify az once 40 milyon dolar topladi. Dogrudan rakibiniz. Daha derin ceplere sahip birine karsi esit sartlari nasil saglarsIniz?

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Great point. Logistify has capital, but they're putting all their eggs in one basket — a single-market strategy in Indonesia. We've diversified across three markets. Their approach is a steep learning curve every time they expand. Ours is a repeatable playbook. We've already bridged the gap between local market knowledge and scalable technology.
lines.textTr: Harika nokta. Logistify'in sermayesi var ama tum riskini tek yere yatiriyorlar — Endonezya'da tek pazar stratejisi. Biz uc pazara cesitlendirdik. Onlarin yaklasimi her genislemede zor bir ogrenme sureci. Bizimki tekrarlanabilir bir oyun plani. Yerel pazar bilgisi ile olceklenebilir teknoloji arasindaki arayi coktan kapattik.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: If I may add — the regulatory landscape in Southeast Asia is challenging. Delays are par for the course. But unlike Logistify, we have on-the-ground teams in each market. Nothing falls through the cracks because we have local expertise managing compliance.
lines.textTr: Ekleyebilirsem — Guneydogu Asya'daki duzenleyici ortam zorlu. Gecikmeler beklenen bir durum. Ama Logistify'dan farkli olarak her pazarda sahadA ekiplerimiz var. Hicbir sey gozden kacmaz cunku uyumlulugu yoneten yerel uzmanligimiz var.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Let me read between the lines here. You're saying your competitive advantage is execution, not just technology. But execution depends on people. What happens if your key talent leaves?
lines.textTr: Burada satirlarin arasini okuyayim. Rekabet avantajinizin sadece teknoloji degil uygulama oldugunu soyluyorsunuz. Ama uygulama insanlara bagli. Kilit yetenekleriniz ayrilirsa ne olur?

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: We don't burn bridges, and we don't throw people under the bus. Our retention rate is 94% — well above the industry average. Why? Because every team member has equity, which means everyone has skin in the game. We've created a culture where people feel accountable AND valued.
lines.textTr: Kopru yakmayiz ve insanlari gunah kecisi yapmayiz. Elde tutma oranImiz %94 — sektor ortalamasinin cok uzerinde. Neden? Cunku her ekip uyesinin hisse payi var, yani herkesin oyunda paylari var. Insanlarin hem sorumlu hem degerli hissettigi bir kultur yarattik.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: I need to raise the stakes on a financial question. Your projections show profitability in 18 months. But what if the economic downturn continues? How do you weather the storm with an 18-month runway?
lines.textTr: Finansal bir soruda bahisleri yukseltmem gerekiyor. Projeksiyonlariniz 18 ayda karlilik gosteriyor. Ama ekonomik durgunluk devam ederse? 18 aylik bir pistle firtinayI nasil atlatirsiniz?

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Fair challenge, David. We have three layers of protection. First, our unit economics are already positive in Thailand — revenue exceeds costs per user. Second, we've built systems to separate the wheat from the chaff in our product pipeline — we only invest in features that move the needle. Third, we have a contingency plan that extends our runway to 24 months by reducing non-essential spending.
lines.textTr: Adil bir meydan okuma David. Uc katli korumamiz var. Birincisi, Tayland'da birim ekonomimiz zaten pozitif — kullanici basina gelir maliyetleri asiyor. Ikincisi, urun hattimizda iyiyi kotuden ayiran sistemler kurduk — sadece gozle gorulur fark yaratan ozelliklere yatirim yapiyoruz. Ucuncusu, zorunlu olmayan harcamalari azaltarak pistimizi 24 aya uzatan bir acil durum planimiz var.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: I appreciate the transparency. One more concern — the client keeps shifting the goalposts in your biggest contract. That $8 million deal with SiamTrade. What's happening there?
lines.textTr: Seffafligi takdir ediyorum. Bir endise daha — musteri en buyuk sozlesmenizdE kurallari surekli degistiriyor. SiamTrade ile olan 8 milyon dolarlik anlasma. Orada ne oluyor?

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: You've done your homework, Ms. Whitfield. Yes, SiamTrade shifted the goalposts twice on deliverables. But here's how we handled it: instead of accepting scope creep, we renegotiated terms. The new contract is actually 15% larger — $9.2 million — because we turned their additional requirements into billable modules. We turned the tide on what could have been a losing situation.
lines.textTr: Odevini yapmissin Bayan Whitfield. Evet, SiamTrade teslimatLarda iki kez kurallari degistirdi. Ama biz nasil ele aldik: kapsam kaymasini kabul etmek yerine sartlari yeniden muzakere ettik. Yeni sozlesme aslinda %15 daha buyuk — 9.2 milyon dolar — cunku ek gereksinimlerini faturalandirIlabilir modullere donusturduk. Kayip bir durum olabilecek seyde gidisati degistirdik.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: That's actually impressive negotiation. Most startups would have just drunk the Kool-Aid and accepted whatever the client demanded.
lines.textTr: Bu aslinda etkileyici bir muzakere. Cogu startup koru korune inanir ve musterinin ne istedigini kabul ederdi.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: We don't drink the Kool-Aid on anything — not client demands, not market hype, not our own projections. Every decision goes through rigorous data analysis. That's how we avoid barking up the wrong tree.
lines.textTr: Hicbir seye koru korune inanmayiz — ne musteri taleplerine, ne piyasa heyecanina, ne kendi projeksiyonlarimiza. Her karar titiz veri analizinden gecer. Yanlis yerde aramaktan boyle kaciniyoruz.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Final question. Doubling your market presence in 12 months while maintaining profitability — that's a tall order. Give me one reason this isn't just another ambitious startup pitch.
lines.textTr: Son soru. Karililigini korurken 12 ayda pazar varliginizi ikiye katlamak — bu zor bir talep. Bunun sadece bir iddiali startup sunumu olmadigina dair bana bir neden verin.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Because we've already done it once. Thailand was our proof of concept. We moved the needle in 90 days, weathered a regulatory storm in month four, and achieved profitability by month nine. The silver lining of starting in the hardest market first is that every subsequent market is easier. We've already gotten our ducks in a row. Now it's time to execute.
lines.textTr: Cunku zaten bir kez yaptik. Tayland konsept kanitimizdi. 90 gunde gozle gorulur fark yarattik, dorduncu ayda duzenleyici firtinayI atlattik ve dokuzuncu aya karliliga ulastik. En zor pazarda baslamanin iyi tarafi her sonraki pazarin daha kolay olmasidir. Her seyi zaten duzenledik. Simdi uygulama zamani.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Well, I must say — you didn't just address every concern, you turned each one into a strength. Team, I've heard enough. Let's discuss terms. NovaTech, I believe we can do business together.
lines.textTr: Su soylemeliyim — sadece her endiseyi ele almadin, her birini bir guce donusturdun. Ekip, yeterince dinledim. Sartlari tartisalim. NovaTech, birlikte is yapabilecegimize inaniyorum.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Thank you, Ms. Whitfield. We look forward to building something extraordinary together. And I promise — we won't shift the goalposts on our commitments.
lines.textTr: Tesekkurler Bayan Whitfield. Birlikte olaganustu bir sey insa etmeyi dort gozle bekliyoruz. Ve soz veriyorum — taahhutlerimizde kurallari degistirmeyecegiz.

questions.question: How did Eyüp demonstrate 'having skin in the game' during the pitch?
questions.questionTr: Eyup sunum sirasinda 'oyunda paylari oldugunu' nasil gostermisti?
options: By investing $200,000 of his own capital

questions.question: What was 'the elephant in the room' that Ms. Whitfield raised?
questions.questionTr: Bayan Whitfield'in gundeme getirdigi 'herkesin bildigi sorun' neydi?

questions.question: How did Eyüp 'turn the tide' on the SiamTrade situation?
options: He renegotiated terms into a 15% larger contract

questions.question: What was Logistify's strategic weakness according to Eyüp?
questions.questionTr: Eyup'a gore Logistify'in stratejik zayifligi neydi?
options: Putting all eggs in one basket — single-market strategy

questions.question: What was 'the silver lining' of starting in the hardest market first?
questions.questionTr: En zor pazarda baslamanin 'iyi tarafi' neydi?
